Here is the summary of all 11 papers, with relevance to variant **rs12979860 (IL28B rs12979860)** indicated for each:

---

### PMC3282030.md
**Title:** The Correlation of IL28B Genotype With Sustained Virologic Response In Romanian Patients With Chronic Hepatitis C
**Relevant to rs12979860:** YES
**Drugs discussed:** Pegylated interferon + ribavirin (standard of care for HCV)
**Phenotypes/Conditions:** Chronic hepatitis C, sustained virologic response (SVR), complete early virologic response (cEVR)
**Summary:** Retrospective study of 107 Romanian patients with chronic hepatitis C treated with pegylated IFN/ribavirin. Patients with the IL28B rs12979860 C/C genotype had significantly higher SVR rates (73.1%) compared to non-C/C genotypes (43.7%, p=0.0126). The C/C genotype also correlated with higher complete early virologic response (cEVR) rates.

---

### PMC3653303.md
**Title:** IL28B Genotype is Associated with Differential Expression of Intrahepatic Interferon-Stimulated Genes in Chronic Hepatitis C Patients
**Relevant to rs12979860:** YES
**Drugs discussed:** Pegylated interferon + ribavirin
**Phenotypes/Conditions:** Chronic hepatitis C, interferon-stimulated gene (ISG) expression, HCV treatment outcome
**Summary:** Investigates whether IL28B genotype explains the relationship between hepatic ISG expression and HCV treatment outcome. The favorable (good response) IL28B variant was strongly associated with lower intrahepatic ISG expression, independent of miR-122 expression. The IL28B genotype was not associated with intrahepatic IL28B mRNA expression in vivo. rs12979860 is specifically examined as a key IL28B SNP.

---

### PMC3674704.md
**Title:** IL28B Expression Depends on a Novel TT/-G Polymorphism Which Improves HCV Clearance Prediction
**Relevant to rs12979860:** YES
**Drugs discussed:** Pegylated interferon + ribavirin
**Phenotypes/Conditions:** Chronic hepatitis C, spontaneous HCV clearance, HCV treatment response
**Summary:** Identifies a novel dinucleotide variant (TT/-G, encompassing rs67272382 and rs74597329) near the IL28B locus, in strong linkage disequilibrium with rs12979860 (R2=0.91). This new variant negatively affects induction of IL28B expression and has strong predictive value for both HCV treatment response and spontaneous resolution, in some cases outperforming rs12979860 as a predictor.

---

### PMC3779247.md
**Title:** Influence of Vitamin D-Related Gene Polymorphisms (CYP27B and VDR) on the Response to Interferon/Ribavirin Therapy in Chronic Hepatitis C
**Relevant to rs12979860:** YES (used as a covariate/confounder in multivariate analysis)
**Drugs discussed:** Pegylated interferon (Peg-IFN) + ribavirin (RBV)
**Phenotypes/Conditions:** Chronic hepatitis C, SVR, therapeutic failure
**Summary:** Evaluates vitamin D-related gene polymorphisms (CYP27B rs10877012, VDR rs2228570, rs1544410, rs7975232, rs731236) as predictors of response to Peg-IFN/RBV in 238 Caucasian CHC patients. IL28B rs12979860 was genotyped and included as a known strong predictor (T allele carrier OR=8.724, p<0.001 for therapy failure) in multivariate models. VDR gene polymorphisms were found to be independently associated with treatment outcome, adding to predictive value alongside IL28B rs12979860.

---

### PMC3793390.md
**Title:** A Variant Upstream of IFNL3 (IL28B) Creating a Novel Interferon Gene IFNL4 is Associated with Impaired Clearance of Hepatitis C Virus
**Relevant to rs12979860:** YES (central to the paper)
**Drugs discussed:** Not a treatment study; biological mechanism study; HCV clearance context
**Phenotypes/Conditions:** Chronic HCV infection, HCV clearance (spontaneous and treatment-induced), liver cirrhosis and cancer risk
**Summary:** Discovers a novel dinucleotide frameshift variant (ss469415590, TT/deltaG) upstream of IL28B (IFNL3) that creates a new gene, IFNL4 (interferon lambda 4). This variant is in high linkage disequilibrium with rs12979860 and is more strongly associated with HCV clearance in individuals of African ancestry. IFNL4 induces STAT1/STAT2 phosphorylation and ISG expression. The study directly compares the predictive performance of ss469415590 with rs12979860 for HCV clearance.

---

### PMC3991683.md
**Title:** IFNL4 ss469415590 Variant Shows Similar Performance to rs12979860 as Predictor of Response to Treatment Against Hepatitis C Virus Genotype 1 or 4 in Caucasians
**Relevant to rs12979860:** YES (direct head-to-head comparison)
**Drugs discussed:** Pegylated interferon (pegIFN) + ribavirin (RBV)
**Phenotypes/Conditions:** Chronic hepatitis C (genotype 1 or 4), SVR prediction
**Summary:** In 272 Caucasian HCV genotype 1/4-infected patients treated with pegIFN/RBV, both rs12979860 and ss469415590 (IFNL4 variant) were genotyped. The study finds that the IFNL4 variant ss469415590 performs comparably to rs12979860 in predicting SVR in Caucasians, with similar area under the ROC curve. Neither marker adds significant incremental predictive value over the other in this population.

---

### PMC4177494.md
**Title:** IL28B Polymorphism Genotyping as Predictor of Rapid Virologic Response During Interferon Plus Ribavirin Treatment in Hepatitis C Virus Genotype 1 Patients
**Relevant to rs12979860:** YES
**Drugs discussed:** Pegylated interferon alpha 2a or 2b + ribavirin; also discusses boceprevir and telaprevir (NS3/4A protease inhibitors) in context
**Phenotypes/Conditions:** Chronic hepatitis C genotype 1, rapid virologic response (RVR), null-response, SVR
**Summary:** In 118 Caucasian HCV-G1 patients treated with pegIFN/RBV, both IL28B SNPs rs12979860 and rs8099917 were genotyped. The rs8099917 G allele was the strongest predictor of null-response at week 4 (OR=7.9). The study shows combined genotyping for both SNPs provides superior risk stratification. rs12979860 CC had a PPV >90% for achieving an HCV RNA reduction at week 4. Concludes that combined IL28B genotyping aids patient management decisions regarding dual vs. triple therapy.

---

### PMC4332701.md
**Title:** Host Genetic Factors and Dendritic Cell Responses Associated with the Outcome of Interferon/Ribavirin Treatment in HIV-1/HCV Co-Infected Individuals
**Relevant to rs12979860:** YES
**Drugs discussed:** PEGylated interferon (PEG-IFN) + ribavirin (RBV); HAART (highly active antiretroviral treatment) for HIV-1; direct-acting antivirals (DAAs) mentioned in context
**Phenotypes/Conditions:** HIV-1/HCV co-infection, SVR, dendritic cell (DC) function, T cell responses
**Summary:** Investigates host genetic factors (including IL28B rs12979860) and dendritic cell phenotypes in HIV-1/HCV co-infected individuals undergoing IFN/RBV treatment. Pre-treatment myeloid DC (mDC) frequencies were lower in non-responders vs. responders. The IL28B rs12979860 CC genotype was included as a host genetic predictor of SVR alongside immunological parameters, highlighting the interplay between IL28B genotype, DC function, and treatment outcome.

---

### PMC4480925.md
**Title:** A Randomized Placebo-controlled Trial to Test a Genetically-informed Biomarker For Personalizing Treatment for Tobacco Dependence
**Relevant to rs12979860:** NO
**Drugs discussed:** Nicotine patch (Nicoderm CQ), varenicline, bupropion (mentioned), placebo
**Phenotypes/Conditions:** Tobacco dependence, smoking cessation, nicotine metabolism (CYP2A6 activity via nicotine metabolite ratio, NMR)
**Summary:** This paper is entirely unrelated to IL28B or rs12979860. It is a pharmacogenetic randomized controlled trial testing whether the nicotine metabolite ratio (NMR; a biomarker of CYP2A6 activity) can personalize smoking cessation treatment. Normal metabolizers benefit more from varenicline than nicotine patch; slow metabolizers derive similar benefit from either. rs12979860 is not mentioned.

---

### PMC4560372.md
**Title:** Association Between CXCL10 and DPP4 Gene Polymorphisms and a Complementary Role for Unfavorable IL28B Genotype in Prediction of Treatment Response in Thai Patients with Chronic Hepatitis C Virus Infection
**Relevant to rs12979860:** YES
**Drugs discussed:** Pegylated interferon-alpha (PEG-IFN-alpha 2a or 2b) + ribavirin; direct-acting antivirals (DAAs) mentioned in context
**Phenotypes/Conditions:** Chronic hepatitis C (genotype 1 and non-genotype 1), SVR, RVR, EVR, liver fibrosis
**Summary:** In 602 Thai CHC patients, IL28B rs12979860 CC genotype was the strongest independent predictor of SVR (OR=3.12, p<0.001) in multivariate analysis alongside HCV RNA level, age, and fibrosis stage. The study also evaluated CXCL10 rs56061981 and DPP4 SNPs. While CXCL10 and DPP4 SNPs did not independently predict SVR, the CXCL10 rs56061981 non-GG genotype complemented unfavorable IL28B rs12979860 CT/TT genotypes in predicting SVR in HCV genotype 1 patients.

---

### PMC4612590.md
**Title:** Pharmacogenetic Pathway Analysis of Docetaxel Elimination
**Relevant to rs12979860:** NO
**Drugs discussed:** Docetaxel (chemotherapy agent), erythromycin (used in breath test for CYP3A activity), midazolam
**Phenotypes/Conditions:** Docetaxel pharmacokinetics/clearance, cancer treatment (solid tumors), CYP3A4/CYP3A5 enzyme activity
**Summary:** This paper is entirely unrelated to IL28B or rs12979860. It investigates the pharmacogenetics of docetaxel elimination, identifying OATP1B3 (SLCO1B3) as the most efficient influx transporter, and finding that the co-presence of CYP3A4*1B and CYP3A5*1A alleles is associated with a 64% increase in docetaxel clearance. No mention of rs12979860 or hepatitis C.
